name: Victor 2017 Tadalafil Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy Phase 3 Extract
population_or_context: Boys with Duchenne muscular dystrophy taking glucocorticoids.
methods: Phase 3 randomized placebo-controlled 48-week trial of tadalafil 0.3 or 0.6 mg/kg/day on 6-minute walk distance and ambulatory-function outcomes.
population_profile: Pediatric boys with DMD; low directness for adult healthy men but important for physical-function claims built on skeletal-muscle perfusion mechanisms.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 0.3 or 0.6 mg/kg/day once daily for 48 weeks.
safety_findings: Adverse events were consistent with known tadalafil safety profile and DMD disease state in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Very low for healthy adult men; useful as a disease-specific negative translation boundary.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Pediatric rare disease, glucocorticoid background therapy, disease progression endpoint, and limited transfer to healthy performance.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but DMD disease biology and pediatric dosing do not transfer to healthy-user performance claims.
